fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with high margins and strong analyst conviction, though valuation is stretched.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 4.6%; next earnings date is 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY and analyst estimates for current Q and FY are exceptionally high.
Operating margin of 21.8% and profit margin of 17.7% indicate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 22.8% shows efficient use of shareholder investments.
Mean analyst target of $1104.68 suggests significant upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 151.59 and Price/Sales of 26.73 indicate a very high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 suggests significant leverage.
Free cash flow of $93.35M is low relative to market cap.
Recent price action shows a pullback with optics stocks cooling off.

news.paragraph: Lumentum is planning a significant expansion with a new 240,000-square-foot AI laser facility in North Carolina, signaling strong demand for its products. However, recent news also shows a notable pullback in optics stocks, with LITE falling 4-4.6% in late May, and a registered bearish activity by an insider. Despite outperforming Nvidia year-to-date, the stock's recent price action and valuation metrics suggest a mixed outlook.
